recipes.title: Meat Pie
recipes.description: Classic Australian hand-sized pie filled with minced beef in rich gravy under a flaky pastry top.
steps: Saute diced onion in oil until translucent, then add minced beef and brown thoroughly.
Stir in flour and cook for one minute, then pour in beef stock, tomato sauce and Worcestershire.
Simmer the filling for 15 minutes until thick, then season and cool completely.
Line individual pie tins with shortcrust pastry and fill with the cooled mince.
Top with puff pastry, trim the edges and brush with beaten egg.
Bake at 200 C for 25 to 30 minutes until the pastry is deep golden.
Rest for five minutes and serve with extra tomato sauce on top.

recipes.title: Lamington
recipes.description: Square sponge cakes dipped in chocolate icing and rolled in desiccated coconut.
steps: Cream butter and caster sugar until pale, then beat in eggs one at a time.
Fold in flour alternately with milk to make a smooth batter.
Pour into a lined slice tin and bake at 180 C for 25 minutes.
Cool completely, then cut into 5 cm squares and chill until firm.
Sift icing sugar with cocoa, whisk in boiling water until glossy.
Dip each cube of cake into the chocolate icing using two forks.
Roll immediately in desiccated coconut and set on a wire rack to dry.

recipes.title: Pavlova
recipes.description: Crisp-shelled meringue with marshmallow centre, topped with whipped cream and fresh fruit.
steps: Whisk egg whites to soft peaks, then add caster sugar a spoonful at a time until glossy.
Fold in vinegar, cornflour and vanilla until just combined.
Mound the meringue onto a lined tray in a 22 cm circle, smoothing the sides.
Bake at 120 C for 1 hour 15 minutes, then turn off the oven and cool inside with the door ajar.
Whip cream to soft peaks and pile generously on top of the cooled meringue.
Decorate with sliced strawberries, kiwifruit rounds and passionfruit pulp.
Serve immediately so the shell stays crisp.

recipes.title: Vegemite Sandwich
recipes.description: Iconic Australian sandwich of buttered white bread spread thinly with salty Vegemite.
steps: Spread softened butter generously over both slices of fresh white bread.
Spread a thin scrape of Vegemite over the buttered side of one slice.
Press the second slice butter side down on top to close the sandwich.
Cut diagonally and serve immediately for breakfast or lunch.

recipes.title: Anzac Biscuits
recipes.description: Chewy oat and golden syrup biscuits originally baked for soldiers during World War I.
steps: Combine oats, flour, coconut and brown sugar in a large bowl.
Melt butter with golden syrup in a small saucepan over low heat.
Mix bicarbonate of soda with boiling water and stir into the butter mixture, which will foam.
Pour the wet mixture over the dry ingredients and stir to a stiff dough.
Roll tablespoons of dough into balls, place on lined trays and flatten slightly.
Bake at 160 C for 15 minutes until golden but still soft in the centre.
Cool on the trays for 10 minutes so they crisp up before transferring to a rack.

recipes.title: Damper
recipes.description: Traditional Australian soda bread historically baked by drovers and stockmen in campfire coals.
steps: Sift flour and salt into a bowl, then rub in cold butter until the mixture resembles breadcrumbs.
Make a well in the centre and pour in most of the milk and water.
Stir with a butter knife to form a soft, slightly sticky dough, adding remaining liquid if needed.
Turn onto a floured surface and shape into a round about 4 cm thick.
Place on a floured tray, score a deep cross on top and dust with extra flour.
Bake at 200 C for 30 minutes until golden and hollow sounding when tapped.
Tear apart while warm and serve with butter and golden syrup.

recipes.title: Sausage Roll
recipes.description: Flaky puff pastry wrapped around seasoned pork sausage filling, an Australian bakery staple.
steps: Grate the onion and carrot finely, then squeeze out any excess liquid.
Combine mince, vegetables, breadcrumbs, one egg and the sauces, season well and mix thoroughly.
Cut each pastry sheet in half and pipe a log of mince along the long edge.
Roll up snugly, seam side down, and cut into 6 cm pieces.
Place on lined trays, brush with the remaining beaten egg and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Bake at 200 C for 25 minutes until the pastry is puffed and deep golden.
Serve hot with tomato sauce on the side.

recipes.title: Fairy Bread
recipes.description: Children's party staple of buttered white bread covered in rainbow hundreds and thousands.
steps: Spread softened butter to the very edges of each slice of fresh white bread.
Tip a generous layer of hundreds and thousands onto a flat plate.
Press each slice butter-side down firmly into the sprinkles to coat completely.
Cut each slice diagonally into four small triangles and arrange on a serving plate.

recipes.title: Chiko Roll
recipes.description: Deep-fried Australian savoury roll inspired by the Chinese spring roll, sold at takeaway shops.
steps: Cook barley in salted water until tender and drain well.
Finely chop cabbage, celery, carrot and onion, then saute in oil until softened.
Add mutton mince and cook through, stirring in soy sauce and cooked barley.
Season generously and cool the filling completely.
Spoon a heaped portion of filling onto each thick wrapper and roll into a fat cigar, sealing the seam with water.
Heat oil to 180 C and deep fry the rolls in batches for 5 minutes until deep golden and crisp.
Drain on paper towel and serve hot.

recipes.title: Dim Sim
recipes.description: Oversized Australian-Chinese steamed or fried dumpling filled with pork and cabbage.
steps: Finely chop cabbage, salt lightly, leave for 10 minutes then squeeze out the moisture.
Combine pork, drained cabbage, spring onion, ginger, soy, sesame oil and white pepper.
Place a heaped tablespoon of filling in the centre of each wrapper.
Gather the wrapper around the filling, leaving the top open like a small cup.
Steam over boiling water for 12 minutes until cooked through.
For the fried version, shallow fry the steamed dim sims in hot oil until golden on all sides.
Serve with soy sauce for dipping.

recipes.title: Carpetbag Steak
recipes.description: Thick beef fillet stuffed with fresh oysters, a retro Australian steakhouse classic.
steps: Cut a deep horizontal pocket into the side of each thick fillet steak.
Tuck three shucked oysters and a small knob of butter into each pocket and secure with a toothpick.
Season the outside generously with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a heavy pan over high heat and sear the steaks for three minutes per side for medium rare.
Rest the steaks under foil for five minutes.
Melt remaining butter with lemon juice and chopped parsley to make a quick sauce.
Remove the toothpicks and spoon the lemon butter over the steaks to serve.

recipes.title: Barramundi
recipes.description: Pan seared whole barramundi fillet, a native Australian fish, finished with lemon and herbs.
steps: Pat the barramundi fillets very dry and score the skin lightly.
Season generously with salt and pepper on both sides.
Heat olive oil in a heavy pan and place the fillets skin side down, pressing for 30 seconds.
Cook undisturbed for four minutes until the skin is crisp and golden.
Flip the fillets, add butter, smashed garlic and a squeeze of lemon, then baste for two minutes.
Remove the fish to warm plates and spoon over the pan butter.
Scatter with chopped parsley and serve with lemon wedges.
